title: 01. Introduction
summary: This repository defines around 30 Skills with the ywc-* prefix under claude-code/skills/ , plus several dedicated Agents under claude-code/agents/ .

title: 02. Core concepts
summary: Once you understand the terms and concepts on this page, you can apply the examples from every other guide page directly.

title: 03. Ship your first feature in 5 minutes
summary: This page is your first hands-on exercise: follow one small bug fix from the idea stage to merge.

title: 04. Handling a small change (general cycle - small)
summary: When ywc-plan judges a request as Small , it automatically enters this path.

title: 05. Handling work split into multiple Tasks (general cycle - medium/large)
summary: If any of the following applies, ywc-plan routes to this path.

title: 06. Finish automatically from one goal (ywc-agentic)
summary: 04 and 05 are manual control flows where the user invokes each step one by one ( ywc-plan -> ...

title: 07. Starting a new Project
summary: Use this when there is no Git repository yet, or when the repository exists but is essentially a blank slate with little code.

title: 08. Entering an existing Repo for the first time
summary: Use this when you first enter an unfamiliar repository that already has code and need to understand "what this codebase looks like." Conversely, if you are designing a new project from a blank slate, go to 07.

title: 09. Writing and running Tests
summary: Use this after feature implementation is done and you need to verify that it really works as intended.

title: 10. E2E Test automation strategy
summary: Use ywc-e2e-test-strategy when the project does not yet have Playwright-based E2E tests, or when existing coverage is scattered and it is unclear "which flow should be automated first." The goal is to automate repeatedly regressing critical flows, such as login or checkout, at minimum cost.

title: 11. Reviewing and improving design
summary: The two Skills answer different questions.

title: 12. Debugging and incident postmortems
summary: The two Skills answer different questions.

title: 13. Managing Code Structure and Maintainability
summary: All four Skills start from the same symptom — "the code is bad" — but answer different questions.

title: 14. Managing Cloud Infrastructure
summary: All four Skills cover one continuous lifecycle — designing, authoring, reviewing, and improving cloud infrastructure — but each answers a different question.

title: 15. Executor / Code-gen Prompt patterns
summary: ywc-sequential-executor , ywc-parallel-executor , and ywc-code-gen are the three Skills whose many options make you repeatedly look up exact syntax.

title: 16. Full Skill Reference
summary: This page groups the remaining Skills not covered in the previous workflow guides by what you want to do .

title: 17. Prerequisites and installation
summary: To use ywc-* Skills reliably, a few tools need to be in place beforehand.
